description: Write comprehensive unit tests for all server actions (app/actions.ts, app/login/actions.ts, app/signup/actions.ts) by mocking the Supabase client and Next.js server-only modules (next/cache, next/navigation).
steps: Create __tests__/actions.test.ts to test all 5 server actions in app/actions.ts: createTask, updateTask, deleteTask, toggleTask, signOut
Mock @/lib/supabase/server (createClient) to return a mock Supabase client with chainable methods: .from().insert(), .from().update().eq(), .from().delete().eq(), .auth.getUser(), .auth.signOut()
Mock next/cache (revalidatePath) and next/navigation (redirect) - redirect should throw a special error to simulate Next.js redirect behavior

Test createTask: (a) returns error when title is empty/whitespace, (b) redirects to /login when user is not authenticated, (c) returns error when supabase insert fails, (d) calls revalidatePath and returns success on valid input
Test updateTask: (a) returns error when title is empty/whitespace, (b) returns error when supabase update fails, (c) calls revalidatePath and returns success on valid input
Test deleteTask: (a) returns error when supabase delete fails, (b) calls revalidatePath and returns success
Test toggleTask: (a) returns error when supabase update fails, (b) calls revalidatePath and returns success with correct is_complete value
Test signOut: calls supabase.auth.signOut() and redirects to /login

Create __tests__/login-actions.test.ts to test app/login/actions.ts login function: (a) returns error when email or password is missing, (b) returns error when signInWithPassword fails, (c) redirects to / on success

Create __tests__/signup-actions.test.ts to test app/signup/actions.ts signup function: (a) returns error when email or password is missing, (b) returns error when signUp fails, (c) redirects to /login?signup=success on success
Run 'source /root/.nvm/nvm.sh && npx vitest run' to confirm all action tests pass
acceptance_criteria: All server action tests pass with 'npx vitest run'
Each action has at least a happy path test and relevant error path tests
Mocks correctly simulate the Supabase client chainable API pattern
redirect() behavior is properly handled (it throws in Next.js)
Tests validate actual behavior (return values, side effects) not just that functions exist
